description: The one declared schema. Every script in scripts/ and every skill/primitive reads contracts from here; nothing redefines them locally.

term.slug_rule: lowercase; collapse runs of non-alphanumerics to single hyphens; strip leading/trailing hyphens
term.definition_rule: 1–2 sentences stating what the concept IS — never what the system does with it; project-specific concepts only, general engineering vocabulary never earns a term
term.avoid_line: optional '_Avoid_: <word>, <word>' line under the definition naming rejected synonyms — the vocabulary is opinionated: one best word, the rest rejected by name; the idiom test (primitives/communicate.md) records a rejected ambient idiom here
term.register: optional '_Register_: <word-family> — <exemplar words>. Reject <rejected register>.' line, declared once per context (on the context's own anchor or as a glossary preamble), naming the word-family the context's vocabulary is drawn from. It is the target the idiom test (primitives/communicate.md) checks a term against: a term from ambient software/networking/business idiom rather than the declared register is a candidate the writer must replace or justify. Absent a declaration the test falls back to the universal rule — reject the ambient idiom, prefer the plain word. Enforcement is convention, never mechanism: no gate rules prose idiomatic.
term.slug_retirement: a term-<slug> id is never repurposed for a different concept — retire the term and mint a new slug, never rebind
term.coverage_rule: every load-bearing vocabulary the system actually uses earns a term — in particular EVERY schema enum that governs behavior (cure_class, timing, drift_kind, enforcement, run_mode, work_order_state, triage_roles, recommendation_strength, lens). A behavior-governing enum with no CONTEXT term is an undocumented-vocabulary gap the probe flags; the design must not lean on a word it never defines.

adr.element_form: under the ADR's first `# ` title
adr.lockstep: the NNNN appears identically in filename, anchor id, and every reference
adr.evolution: append-only ledger — a decision is superseded by a new ADR that links the old one, never rewritten; the design document and CONTEXT are present-tense snapshots, the ADR set is the history
adr.body_rule: the title IS the decision statement (no 'ADR-NNNN:' prefix in the title text — the number lives in filename and anchor); body is 1–3 sentences: context, decision, why — a single paragraph is a complete ADR; considered-options / consequences sections only when the rejected alternatives or non-obvious consequences are genuinely worth remembering, never template filler
qualifies: technology selections with real trade-offs or lock-in
constraints invisible in the system itself
surprising conventions a reader would otherwise 'fix'
reversals of earlier decisions (the superseding ADR)

homing.rule: the design layer lives ENTIRELY under docs/ — every layer basename has a declared home, and a layer file found outside its home is a mishomed stray (layer-integrity checks this mechanically). The design references the system by path; the system never hosts layer files.
homing.note: <context> is exactly ONE path segment matching context_segment — the context's kebab-case name (a context folder under docs/design/ holds only that context's layer files). ADR homing is owned by adr_dir + anchors.adr.filename_pattern, not listed here.

multi_context.context_home: each context homes its design.md (+ generated design.html) + CONTEXT.md under docs/design/<context>/ — never colocated in a source directory (see homing)
multi_context.context_map: docs/CONTEXT-MAP.md links every CONTEXT.md as an exact markdown link, each with a one-line description; a relationships section declares cross-context relationships in the DDD vocabulary (customer/supplier, conformist, anti-corruption layer, shared kernel, separate ways) with direction; a shared term names exactly one owning context — others link to the owner, never redefine; the map owns context-level relationships only — fine-grained structural relations live in the root design document's system-at-a-glance
multi_context.no_root_glossary: in multi-context mode there is no single-context glossary (no docs/design/CONTEXT.md) — every glossary lives in its context folder; a glossary outside one is an orphan
multi_context.root_design_doc: docs/design/design.md links every context design document

single_context.split_trigger: a second real vocabulary, never module count

layer_layout.domain_discovery: domains are DISCOVERED, not declared: cluster the system's entities and components by correlation strength — shared vocabulary, direct interaction, co-change — and form a domain from each strongly correlated cluster with weak external coupling. A discovered domain is an abstraction layer that composes with its peers through named seams. The file-level context split (own design.md + CONTEXT.md) is the STRONGEST form and requires a second real vocabulary; below that threshold discovered domains still structure the design document — its sections, its NN.M disclosure, and its diagrams follow the domain clusters, never an arbitrary outline. Discovery is CONTINUOUS, not creation-only: it runs when a design is created (new-design), when one is derived (sync onboard), and again on every meaningful design update — a delta that adds, moves, or newly couples components re-asks whether the clusters still hold (small deltas rarely change them; structural deltas and large refactorings must reconsider). A split or merge of domains is itself an intent change, led through the layer like any other. The clustering is PROPOSED by the agent and RULED by the user: domain boundaries are semantic understanding — how the owner sees the system — so the agent recommends a granularity with its justification (grilling when the carving is genuinely uncertain), and the user's view decides; under afk the recommendation proceeds and the decision is recorded for review
layer_layout.design_ish_markers_note: entries starting with 're:' are regexes matched against the whole link target (catches adr-ish filenames linked outside docs/adr/ or without a fragment); others: trailing '/' = directory prefix, containing '#' = substring of the destination, else exact basename match

staleness_advisory.note: an ADVISORY, never a check — exit codes untouched: when the repo has moved threshold_commits+ commits since any layer artifact last changed, layer-integrity prints one line naming the count and recommending reconcile (sync). The always-on mechanical form of the standing-directives staleness hint; runs wherever the gate is wired (pre-commit, CI, probes). A script reading an older schema copy without this key skips the advisory silently (tolerate-absent). Skipped outside git and when the layer was never committed.

layer_layout.generation_debris_patterns_note: python-re regexes matched line-by-line against every layer artifact (design.md, CONTEXT.md, CONTEXT-MAP.md, COVERAGE.md, ADRs; fenced code blocks exempt — an example is documentation): a matching line is a generation-wrapper artifact, writer leakage the sole writer must never leave behind. layer-integrity reads this list; extend it here as new wrapper shapes surface, never in the script.

ownership.design_doc: the design itself, in three aspects: the single authoritative snapshot of intent (the true source of design information), human-optimized (progressive disclosure, domain structure, reproduction depth), richly presented. Self-sufficient and concise: links a CONTEXT term where a word needs knowing; links an ADR briefly only where a reader would ask why — never depends on either to state the design
ownership.context: semantics — the canonical definition, one term per anchor, plus every semantic nuance that would clutter the design document; no implementation detail
ownership.adr: metadata to the design — the reasoning, discussion, and history behind one decision per anchor; NEVER contains the design itself, and the design document never depends on it
ownership.sole_writer_rule: a sole writer is minted PER KIND of durable authored artifact — the general form, never a per-repo exception: the design layer's writer is design-write, the system-under-change's writer is work-order. A repo whose durable artifacts include a third kind (e.g. skill definitions, written by write-a-skill) has a third sole writer as an INSTANCE of this rule, not a carve-out. Framing any writer as a 'this-repo exception' leaks the dogfood instance into the generic design.

coverage_map.what: the answer to 'is any part of the system blind to the design'. docs/COVERAGE.md (its declared home — see homing), referenced from the entry points: a table listing every meaningful system part with a status and a pointer.

statuses.captured: a design unit describes this part at reproduction grade — the row links the owning design-document section or context
statuses.standard: intentionally not detailed — evident boilerplate the owner chose not to design (conventional dev tooling, CI, generated files). A recorded, revisitable decision, not a gap. The row states WHY it is standard.
statuses.out-of-scope: not a designed subsystem at all (license, .gitignore, vendored third-party). The row says so.

coverage_map.granularity: one row per meaningful part, at the coarsest honest grain — a whole subsystem, not every file. The agent proposes the part list and each status; the user rules (see completeness.breadth.capture_everything_default).
coverage_map.enforced_by: the conformance probe diffs the system's actual parts against the map: a part present in the system but absent from the map is uncovered drift. review-design walks the map for breadth. Not mechanically enforced by a script yet — the part-enumeration is judgment — so the honest tag is convention until a coverage-lint exists.
coverage_map.revisitable: status is not permanent: a standard part that grows non-standard is promoted to captured through the normal design-change path.
coverage_map.currency: the map is created at onboard and MAINTAINED at every reconcile (not only first generation): the conformance probe's breadth sweep reconciles it against the current system each run — new parts get a status, deleted parts' rows are pruned, standard parts that grew logic are promoted to captured. coder adds a row when a chunk introduces a whole new subsystem. So the map cannot silently rot.
coverage_map.patch_accumulation: DERIVED from git history, never stored: for every captured row, the probe computes the commits touching the row's paths since the row's owning design document last changed (churn-vs-design-age), and reports the ratio on every run exactly like ledger aging — never silently skipped. A row whose churn reaches the flag threshold while its design stays untouched is a patch-accumulation candidate: the probe emits a proposed (design-change, tracked) finding for the owning unit — a PROPOSAL routed like any finding, never an auto-route; feature churn under an honest design is the human's call to dismiss. Flag threshold: 5+ commits with zero design movement over the same span; revisable here, cited by the probe and review-design, never redefined locally.

artifact_filenames.note: the design layer's own file basenames. Every file carrying one of these names is a layer artifact and must sit at its declared home (see homing) — the layer never colocates into source directories, so any tool governing a source directory may treat these basenames like any other stray file.

unit_model.recursive: the design layer is a recursively self-similar TREE of units. A unit is the same shape at every altitude — an overview plus its child units plus a pointer to its parent. 'context', 'component', and an NN.M subsection are depth labels on ONE node type, not different things. The root indexes contexts; a context indexes its components; a component indexes its NN.M sub-parts; the recursion continues until a unit passes the reproduction test (design_doc.completeness).
unit_model.index_children_only: a unit's overview indexes ONLY its own children. A reference to a unit that is not its child is always an explicit POINTER — down to a child, up to an ancestor, or lateral to another unit — never a silent inclusion and never a silent omission. A spanning index (a 'whole set at a glance') that lists some off-node units and drops others is an authoring bug: either point to every off-node unit or scope the section to this unit's own children, and state which. This is the general rule; a domain document half-listing sibling-context-owned units is one instance of violating it.
unit_model.lateral_graph: OVERLAID on the recursive tree is a lateral REFERENCE GRAPH — CONTEXT terms owned by one unit and cited by others, seams between sibling units, and the DDD relationships (the set is owned by layer_layout.multi_context.context_map) recorded in the context map. These lateral edges are NOT the recursion (the tree gives containment/disclosure; the graph gives cross-references) and are not the file split either.
unit_model.three_concerns_distinct: keep three concerns separate, never conflated: (1) RECURSION governs containment and progressive disclosure (this model); (2) the FILE SPLIT — where the recursion breaks into separate design.md/CONTEXT.md files — is domain_discovery's second-real-vocabulary rule, a semantic judgment, not a depth counter; (3) the LATERAL GRAPH is cross-reference. A unit can recurse deeply inside one file (NN.M.K) without a file split, and can hold lateral edges without those being parent/child.

design_doc.source: design.md — authored markdown with custom blocks; the reviewable unit
design_doc.rendered: design.html — generated by scripts/design-render, never hand-edited, committed and promoted alongside its source (freshness proven by regenerate-and-compare)
design_doc.syntax: ::: container directives for semantic blocks (remark/markdown-it style); standard fenced code for mermaid/vega-lite; CONTEXT.md and ADRs stay plain markdown with the anchor contract

generation.pass: validation + typesetting normalization in one pass; fail-closed on unknown blocks, missing or mis-ordered foundation, malformed pending entries, broken diagrams; a design that does not render clean does not promote
generation.link_rewrite: authors write .md links throughout (markdown layer stays self-consistent); when rendering, the generator rewrites every link whose resolved target is a design.md (whole-document navigation) to the sibling design.html, so the HTML layer is self-consistent. Anchor links into a CONTEXT term (.md#term-) or an ADR (.md#adr-) are left unchanged — those anchors live in markdown, with no rendered page. Rewrite is by resolved target basename, fragment-less-or-not: foo/design.md -> foo/design.html; foo/design.md#02-x -> foo/design.html#02-x

blocks.principle: aspirational, abstract value that guides judgment; attrs: id (stable, referencable from prose, e.g. P1), lens — both optional
blocks.invariant: checkable property; attrs: enforcement=mechanism|partial|convention — mechanism and partial must each name their script; lens optional
blocks.pending: pending-ledger entry; attrs: kind=build|verify|foundation|ruling; body: summary, adr link, optional tracker issue
blocks.cards: a grid of cards — the workhorse structure for component maps, principle grids, rationale cards, comparison rows, caption strips; items delimited by ### headings inside the block (body before the first ### is a violation); attrs: cols=2|3|4 (default auto-fit), size=md|sm, tint (optional accent)
blocks.info: callout; attrs: title (optional micro-caps heading), tint (optional)
blocks.svg: embed an SVG from a sibling file; attrs: file
blocks.mermaid: fenced code block — structure diagrams. Validated at build time via mermaid.parse() (the same grammar and version as the client-side render), so a syntax error fails the gate, never the reader's browser. Convention: in-domain nodes classDef-tinted with the context accent, external nodes white with dashed border
blocks.vega-lite: fenced code block — plots, JSON spec

block_fields.principle: block contracts ARE the design language's invariants: each kind declares the information it must capture (fields, attrs, allowed vocabularies), the generator enforces the contract fail-closed at render time, and the writer instructs authors through it. Capturing a NEW kind of information reliably means minting or extending a block contract — schema + renderer + design-write evolve in lockstep, and the change re-validates every existing document. Never capture load-bearing language as free-form prose when a contract can hold it
block_fields.statement_blocks: goal, no-goal, principle, invariant share one anatomy: a required LABEL — bold text alone on the block's first line, ≤64 chars, plain (no links) — the short description; then the BODY — the long description as markdown (required, except optional on no-goal; for invariant the body IS the checkable statement). Badges render in a fixed head row derived from attrs: principle → id chip + lens pill(s); invariant → enforcement chip (+ script chip when a script is named) + lens pill(s); goal/no-goal → lens pill(s) when given. The lens attr accepts a multi-value list (lens=state+composition, same syntax as inline combos), validated against the lens enum
block_fields.rendered_slots: every block renders the same slot structure for the stylist: .entry-head (containing .label and .badges) then .body — cards items, callouts (title = label), and pending entries (kind badge + since = badges) follow the same anatomy
block_fields.validated: field presence, label length and shape, and attr legality are generation-time violations — the structure is enforced by design-render on every render, so architectural changes to the block language always re-validate the whole document

inline.lens_pill: a code span `lens:<name>` renders as that lens's pill; combos `lens:a+b` render one gradient pill with lenses auto-sorted into the fixed enum order; an unknown lens name is a violation

accents.note: the tint axis marks OWNERSHIP (a bounded context keeps one stable accent across all its documents); lens pills mark JUDGMENT — two axes, never conflated

frontmatter_keys.eyebrow: micro-caps identity line above the title — the document's position in the layer hierarchy, with links up (e.g. 'Domain overview · Meta · [root]')
frontmatter_keys.lede: muted one-to-three-line summary under the title; inline markdown
frontmatter_keys.footer: colophon line — ownership split restated, links back up

design_doc.foundation_cardinality: at least one goal, zero or more no-goals, at least one invariant, at least one principle — in that order, all inside section 00. EXEMPTION: a multi-context root that only indexes its child contexts declares 'index_only: true' in frontmatter and is waived from the per-kind minimum — it carries only genuinely cross-context foundation and points down to each context for the rest, never restating a context-owned goal/invariant/principle. The flag is legal only on a document that links at least one child <context>/design.md; on a single-domain document (no child contexts) it is a violation, so a leaf can never skip its own foundation. The order rule still binds whatever foundation the root does carry.
spine: 00 Foundation — goals, no-goals, invariants, principles: each in its block kind, in that order
Pending updates — unnumbered, immediately after the foundation; omitted when empty
02..0N-1 progressive breakdown, gross to fine — recursing via sub-numbered subsections (NN.M) until every unit passes the completeness test

completeness.two_axes: completeness has two distinct axes, both required. BREADTH (coverage): every meaningful system or subsystem appears in the design layer — captured, or explicitly annotated as intentionally-not-detailed. DEPTH (reproduction grade): each captured unit is deep enough to rebuild. Depth without breadth is a layer that describes some parts perfectly and is blind to others; breadth without depth is an index with no design. A completeness pass checks BOTH.

breadth.property: no meaningful part of the system is invisible to the design layer. The question breadth asks: is there any subsystem the design is blind to? Every part is accounted for in the coverage map (layer_layout.coverage_map) with one status — captured, standard, or out-of-scope. An unaccounted part is uncovered drift (drift_kind), never a silent omission.
breadth.capture_everything_default: the default is to capture everything. The deciding question for any part is the reproduction test: would an independent agent, handed the design layer alone, need this part's DESIGN to rebuild an equivalent system? A part carries real logic or non-obvious design (its shape was a decision) -> capture it. A part is plain, conventional infrastructure whose shape a competent builder would reproduce the same way from its mere presence (standard dependency manifests and lockfiles, standard build/test/CI wiring, formatter/linter config, ignore and license files, vendored third-party) -> a candidate for standard. This is GENERIC over any stack — the categories are 'carries logic / design decisions' vs 'vanilla infrastructure', never a fixed tool list; a bespoke or complex build pipeline, a non-trivial dependency-fetching scheme, or custom CI logic is itself design and should be captured. A part is marked standard or out-of-scope ONLY by an explicit decision: the agent proposes the status with its reason ('this looks standard — skip it from the design?'), and the user rules — tracking is always available if the user wants it, and the agent recommends capturing anything that looks less-than-standard. Every mark is explicit and revisitable — a standard part that grows non-standard is promoted to captured. Being explicit, not implicit, is the point.
breadth.boilerplate_note: 'internals derivable from a captured unit (algorithm bodies, boilerplate within it)' are legitimately absent from DEPTH — a depth exclusion inside a covered unit, not a breadth decision. A whole standard subsystem left undetailed is a BREADTH decision recorded in the coverage map.

completeness.test: the reproduction test (the DEPTH axis): a third party holding only the layer — design document, CONTEXT, ADRs, no source — converges on an equivalent implementation of every CAPTURED part: the same components, interfaces, interactions, held invariants, and failure behavior. Internals derivable from those (algorithm bodies, boilerplate) are legitimately absent; abstractions, contracts, state, and interactions never are
completeness.unit_shape: each design unit's subsection answers: what it is responsible for; the interface it exposes; who it interacts with and how; the invariants it holds; how it fails. A STATEFUL unit additionally models its state machine COMPLETELY — the states AND the transitions between them (edges, their guards/triggers, and re-entries) — never just the list of state names. A schema-declared state enum (run_mode, work_order_state, triage_roles) whose transitions live only in an implementation file is a depth gap: the edge set is load-bearing and underivable from the node list alone.
completeness.depth_stop: a section is deep enough exactly when its unit passes the reproduction test — never a page budget, never a fixed level count
completeness.applied_by: sync (onboard scopes coverage with the user first, then derives captured parts to reproduction grade; the probe audits BOTH axes), review-design (breadth pass over the coverage map + per-unit reproduction walk), new-design (the fixpoint includes the intended coverage), incremental updates (a meaningful new subsystem enters the coverage map before it is built)

assumptions.system_home: v3 assumes every system under design lives in a git repo. Future named concept, deliberately undesigned: 'stealth mode' — a companion design repo overlaying another repo/system without injecting skills or design files into it.

pending_ledger.location: one design-document section immediately after the foundation header; omitted entirely when empty; an entry lives in the design document that owns the affected structure
entry: since (YYYY-MM-DD — lets review-design report aging entries as design debt)
adr link (required for build entries; optional for verify/foundation/ruling — they may predate any ADR)

kinds.build: designed, not yet built — cleared by explicit sign-off when the system catches up; independent conformance double-check on request
kinds.verify: derived from the system, faithfulness unverified — cleared mechanically by the first clean conformance probe
kinds.foundation: foundation content the system cannot yield (goals, no-goals, principles) — deferred at bootstrap or underivable at onboarding; cleared by the grilling session that fills it
kinds.ruling: an open owner decision the layer cannot settle; intentional-open to the conformance probe; cleared by the owner's ruling, recorded and applied

pending_ledger.rule: the single home for intentionally-open state; no badges scattered through pages
pending_ledger.link_resolution: design-render requires build entries to carry a link whose fragment matches the adr id pattern (verify/foundation/ruling entries are link-optional — they may predate any ADR); whether a link resolves to a real anchor is layer-integrity's job (entry links are ordinary design-ish links)

cure_class.note: proposed by any skill (harvesters, reviewers, coder); validated and routed only by frontdesk — when no frontdesk is in the loop (direct invocation), the proposing skill presents the list and OFFERS to run it (it never routes itself); a genuine one-off carries none and is dropped. durable-fact = worth remembering, no repo change: frontdesk routes it to agent memory

timing.note: frontdesk's second, orthogonal decision: act in-session or defer to the tracker via to-issues; cure_class says WHAT kind of cure, timing says WHEN; skills propose, frontdesk (or the user, on direct invocation) decides

least_surprise_default.divergent: side that carries the decision weight, not recency
least_surprise_default.bypass: restore the designed seam unless the bypass is the better design
least_surprise_default.uncovered: a meaningful system part is in neither the design nor the coverage map — propose its status (capture it, or mark standard/out-of-scope) and let the user rule; default to capture

enforcement.note: mechanism and partial each name their script (mechanism: the script decides the whole property; partial: the script checks its mechanical shadow); a convention truthfully labelled beats a mechanism falsely claimed

run_mode.rule: derived, never asked: is a human answering now? A decidable failure BLOCKS an afk run; it WARNS-and-offers interactively. Judgment checkpoints (path confirmations, hypothesis re-ranks) never block afk: proceed with the recommended option and record the decision in the run report

work_order_state.note: bounced re-enters dispatched with the same agent (it keeps its context) and the bounce report appended

failure_edges.abandoned: the agent is lost (dead, unresponsive, no delivery) — re-dispatched as a FRESH order whose brief carries the appended history; bounce-same-agent cannot apply to a dead agent
failure_edges.escalated: the brief itself is judged flawed — the orchestrator fixes the brief and re-dispatches; never bounced onto the agent

triage_roles.note: every issue carries exactly one of each; canonical names — the mapping to real label strings lives in the repo config

recommendation_strength.note: stated on every lens-board finding alongside (cure_class, timing); the honest confidence tag — hedging prose is forbidden, the tag carries the uncertainty

lens.note: the order is fixed and load-bearing: combo pills and combo findings list contributing lenses in this order (see primitives/lens-board.md and the style asset). Modeling leads because it is upstream — it carves the entities the other five then judge.

skill_frontmatter.name_rule: name equals the skill's directory name
skill_frontmatter.description_rule: third person; sentence 1 = what it does; then concrete 'Use when …' trigger phrases — the description is the only routing signal the agent sees

status.rule: ship ⇔ listed in README index and .claude-plugin/plugin.json

scripts_contract.fail_closed: anything that LOOKS like a design-ish reference but does not resolve to a known-good shape is a violation, never a silent skip
scripts_contract.location: scripts/ only — never inside a skill or primitive folder
scripts_contract.self_test: every check script ships a fixture-based self-test asserting exit codes
scripts_contract.design_render: the md→html generator doubles as the design document's structural validator; the gate regenerates and compares to prove design.html fresh
scripts_contract.distribution: setup-project-skills copy-installs the checks AND schema/design-schema.json into target repos (scripts/ + hook/CI wiring; layer-integrity reads the schema from its script-sibling copy). Each installed script carries a 'design-schema-version: <N>' stamp near the top (comment leader varies by language — grep the bare substring, never the leader); generated/bundled tools additionally carry a 'design-render-build: <hash>' stamp because behavior can change under an unchanged schema version. sync compares BOTH stamps to the framework's copies via scripts/gate-stamp-check (copy-installed alongside the checks) and warns-and-offers re-install when stale — staleness warns rather than blocks: an old gate still runs, it is just behind. The framework home is DERIVED, never searched for: the executing skill's own SKILL.md is a symlink into the framework repo, so its realpath names the home (the repo root two levels up, the one holding schema/design-schema.json); when a host installed the skill by copy and the derivation fails, ask the user for the framework home once — never scan the filesystem

acceptance_rubric.note: declared once here; cited by work-order (acceptance gate 2) and review-implementation (adherence + craft axes); never re-inlined or forked
checks: honors the inlined design constraints (anchors cited)
tests actually cover the acceptance criteria
introduces no per-case special branch whose general form is unnamed — a one-off adaptation either generalizes or bounces (the taste canon applied at change time, not just review time)
captured in the design layer, or pre-cleared as behavior-only

tracker_writes.ai_disclaimer: every write to an issue tracker carries an AI-disclaimer prefix (text from the repo config)
tracker_writes.deferred_skill_change: a tracked skill-change files in the tracker of the repo that owns the skills (the framework repo), cross-referenced from the repo where it surfaced
tracker_writes.issue_birth: issues enter the tracker as needs-triage — except to-issues may publish issues born ready-for-agent when the publishing session had explicit user sign-off on the slice set. PRDs and other non-work documents are born ready-for-human (they are inputs to slicing, not grabbable work; the ready-for-agent exception is to-issues' alone)

repo_config.home: the fenced 'Agent skills' block homes in AGENTS.md, the cross-agent standard (docs/adr/0015-agents-md-canonical-home.md): AGENTS.md exists → merge the block there and ensure the CLAUDE.md link; neither file exists → create AGENTS.md and the link; only CLAUDE.md exists → merge into it as-is, never migrate silently (hitl: offer the conversion once; afk: merge and report the deviation); never write the block into both files. The link is a committed symlink CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md, unconditional (the framework supports macOS/Linux only) because Claude Code reads only CLAUDE.md (docs-verified 2026-07-08); on detect, an existing CLAUDE.md that is already a symlink to AGENTS.md, or a file whose content imports AGENTS.md (an @AGENTS.md stub), counts as a valid link

block_fence.first_line_inside: (machine-owned; do not edit inside this fence — re-run setup to refresh)
block_fence.refresh_rule: re-runs replace exactly the fenced region and touch nothing outside it; the block carries NO version stamp — consumption-only content has no rot vector

repo_config.content_contract: consumption-only: a short, self-contained description of the framework-derived artifacts, usable by a reader or agent WITHOUT the skills — NO skill names, NO framework-home references, NO behavioral rules; behavior lives in the skills themselves
repo_config.seeded_conventions: repo-authored prose setup-project-skills seeds OUTSIDE the fence (docs/adr/0016-conventions-seeded-outside-the-fence.md): created if absent, owned by the repo afterwards, never refreshed — the fence stays consumption-only. Current set: clean commit messages — no trailers, no attribution, no Co-Authored-By, no 'Generated with' footers
contents: design-layer entry points (CONTEXT-MAP.md → design documents; terms in CONTEXT.md; decisions in docs/adr/) — marked pending when the layer is not yet created
gate run instructions (scripts/design-render --check on each design.md, scripts/layer-integrity .)

staleness hint (skill-free): system moved many commits since the design documents last changed → reconcile design and code before relying on the layer (layer-integrity prints this advisory mechanically past the layer_layout.staleness_advisory threshold)
repo_config.framework_home_grant: setup-project-skills also writes the framework home into the target repo's .claude/settings.json (permissions.additionalDirectories) so composed playbooks stay readable in headless/afk runs — without it, skills cannot load their primitives and MUST stop rather than improvise; the Agent skills block never references the grant (the grant serves the skills, the block serves the reader)

local_tracker.root: issues/ (a config value; this is the default)
local_tracker.roles: each issue file carries two header lines near the top — 'Status: <state-label>', 'Category: <category-label>' — labels per the repo's role-to-label map. Whether a slice needs a human is not a stored label: it is derived at build time from run_mode (a decidable gap blocks an afk run), never predicted per issue
local_tracker.comments: conversation appends to the bottom of the file under a '## Comments' heading; every entry carries the AI-disclaimer prefix (tracker_writes.ai_disclaimer)
local_tracker.publish: create a new file under the feature dir, creating directories as needed
local_tracker.fetch: read the file at the referenced path; the user normally passes the path or issue number

idempotency_protocol.note: the five-step protocol every repo-wiring artifact goes through; cited by setup-project-skills and bootstrap-dev-env — never re-inlined
steps: detect — inspect the repo; never assume
merge — non-destructively if present: add what is missing, never delete or rewrite user content
report — every artifact appears in the final report with the action taken

test_smells.note: decision procedure for the acceptance_rubric item 'tests assert behavior, not implementation'; cited by work-order (gate 2) and review-implementation (craft axis); never re-inlined or forked
red_flags: mocks an internal collaborator (your own module)
asserts call counts or call order
verifies through a side channel instead of the public interface

brief_durability_contract.owner: this schema entry is canonical; primitives/work-order.md binds it for work orders
brief_durability_contract.litmus: a fresh agent with zero memory delivers from the brief alone
rules: inline what the agent must reason from; reference only stable paths (ADR, glossary, config)
never file paths or line numbers — they go stale

primitive_contract.concern: a primitive is a hidden shared playbook owning exactly one concern
primitive_contract.admission: a primitive is admitted only with at least two CONSUMERS — skills or sibling primitives, counted alike; below two consumers the behavior stays skill-local. (e.g. communicate is admitted by its sibling-primitive consumers.)
primitive_contract.bundled_resource: a bundled resource is a file owned by exactly ONE primitive (e.g. primitives/lenses/* owned by lens-board; primitives/assets/*), loaded lazily by its owner and counted as part of that owner — exempt from the two-consumer admission floor, because it is one primitive's material, not a shared concern
primitive_contract.composition: consumers compose a primitive through the relative symlink and never re-inline its concern

modeling_lens.concern: how the system is MODELED — the carving of the domain into entities and their relationships, BEFORE the other lenses judge the resulting structure. Orthogonal to composition (correlated, not overlapping): modeling PRODUCES the parts; composition judges whether those parts stay unbraided. A design can carve the domain wrong yet still be deep/composable/fail-loud — no other lens catches that.
modeling_lens.question: are these the right entities, and are their relationships, transformations, and actions surfaced?
modeling_lens.enemy: mis-carving reality — wrong or missing entities, unsurfaced relationships, unnamed transformations, misplaced actions.
modeling_lens.probe: the DDD decomposition, in order: (1) ENTITIES — the things the domain is made of (entities, value objects, aggregates); (2) RELATIONSHIPS — how they relate (associations, the bounded-context map, DDD relationships, the set owned by layer_layout.multi_context.context_map) — these are part of the lateral graph of layer_layout.unit_model (one of its three edge kinds, alongside CONTEXT-term citations and sibling seams); (3) TRANSFORMATIONS — the maps between entities (how one derives/becomes another); naming these reveals which relationships are load-bearing and drives the design; (4) ACTIONS — the operations on entities and the transformations run on them (domain services, commands); (5) COMPOSITION & RECURSION — how entities compose into larger abstractions, and where composition becomes self-similar (recursion is composition that reuses its own definition — a powerful special case, always asked, never forced; see unit_model).
modeling_lens.ddd_framing: DDD is the DEFAULT and most fundamental modeling frame — even more generic than composition or recursion, because you carve the entities before asking how they compose. It is HIGHLY RECOMMENDED, never enforced: a system that models better as dataflow, types, or a flat set may say so, stating the frame it chose. Recursion and deep composition are powerful outcomes to reach for by reducing entities to smaller composable parts — reached for, not mandated.
modeling_lens.not_this: not 'draw a UML diagram of every class'; not forcing domain vocabulary onto a system without a domain. The lens asks whether the CHOSEN modeling frame carves reality well.
modeling_lens.applied_by: new-design (the modeling method derives the structure — its 'find the structure' step); grill-with-design (re-models when intent changes the entities); review-design (the board's modeling lens judges whether the carving holds).
